creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Ed Vielmetti from comment #1)
Our package builds generally take advantage of multiple cores by building n ports in parallel, but each individual port builds using only one CPU.

For example, a peek at ThunderX1.nyi.freebsd.org shows a build in progress, with the following ports being built in parallel:

01	openobex-1.7.2	comms/openobex	build-depends	00:02:08
02	webkit2-gtk3-2.16.6_3	www/webkit2-gtk3	build	16:48:12
03	geany-plugin-workbench-1.32	devel/geany-plugin-workbench	build-depends	00:00:35
04	solarus-1.5.3_1	games/solarus	build	00:32:34
05	webkit-gtk2-2.4.11_14	www/webkit-gtk2	build	17:07:19
06	ghostscript9-base-9.06_13	print/ghostscript9-base	build	00:23:52
07	firefox-esr-52.5.0_1,1	www/firefox-esr	build	10:22:05
08	gstreamer-plugins-sdl-0.10.23_3,3	devel/gstreamer-plugins-sdl	configure	00:02:04
09	abiword-3.0.1_7	editors/abiword	build	02:25:55
10	firefox-57.0_2,1	www/firefox	build	08:53:19
11	seamonkey-2.49.1_7	www/seamonkey	build	07:04:17
12	vtk5-5.10.1_7	math/vtk5	build	04:23:33
13	llvm-cheri128-6.0.d20171113	devel/llvm-cheri128	build	25:36:10
14	icu-lx-60.1	devel/icu-lx	build	00:19:02
15	simgear-2017.3.1	devel/simgear	build	00:31:32
16	webkit-gtk3-2.4.11_13	www/webkit-gtk3	build	13:23:59
17	thunderbird-52.5.0	mail/thunderbird	build	07:14:27
18	xfce4-dashboard-0.6.1_1	x11/xfce4-dashboard	package	00:10:24
19	libxul-45.9.0_9	www/libxul	build	09:10:44
20	vtk6-6.2.0_5	math/vtk6	build	12:45:27
21	glade-3.18.3_3	devel/glade	build	00:08:05
22	llvm-devel-6.0.d20171113	devel/llvm-devel	build	36:36:22
23	mpv-0.27.0_2,1	multimedia/mpv	build	00:58:26
24	aqsis-1.8.2_15	graphics/aqsis	build	00:56:56

We do have infrastructure to build selected ports with -j (the ALLOW_MAKE_JOBS_PACKAGES list I mentioned above) for ports that take unreasonably long to build w/o -j, and arguably llvm50 ought to be added to that list. But just bumping the maximum timeout would also be sufficient -- we're getting to 95% complete before the job was killed.
